metaTitle: Table Border Styles — Solid, Dashed, Dotted, and More | Tablesmit
metaDescription: Set table borders the way Word does — choose border type, style, and colour. All borders, outside only, inside only, or none. Free, no signup.
heroHeadline: Borders that match your document, not your tool's defaults.
heroSubtext: Choose from solid, dashed, dotted, or double borders. Set them on any selection — all cells, outside edges only, inside dividers only, or a single edge. Pick any colour.

benefits.heading: Every border configuration you need
benefits.body: All borders, outside borders only, inside borders only, top, bottom, left, right, or no border at all. The same options you have in Microsoft Word's table formatting.

benefits.heading: Any border colour
benefits.body: The border colour picker lets you match your brand palette, your document's accent colour, or keep it neutral. Border colour applies independently of cell fill colour.

benefits.heading: Multiple stroke styles
benefits.body: Solid for standard tables. Dashed for draft or provisional data. Dotted for subtle separators. Double for outer borders that need visual weight.

benefits.heading: Exports exactly as set
benefits.body: Border styles render correctly in PDF, PNG, and JPEG export. Excel export preserves border style and colour using native .xlsx border formatting.

borderTypes.name: All Borders
borderTypes.description: Applies a border to every edge of every selected cell.

borderTypes.name: Outside Borders
borderTypes.description: Border on the outer perimeter of the selection only. No inner cell dividers.

borderTypes.name: Inside Borders
borderTypes.description: Border between cells only. No outer edge border.

borderTypes.name: Inside Horizontal
borderTypes.description: Horizontal dividers between rows only. No vertical or outer borders.

borderTypes.name: Inside Vertical
borderTypes.description: Vertical dividers between columns only. No horizontal or outer borders.

borderTypes.name: Top Border
borderTypes.description: Top edge of the selection only.

borderTypes.name: Bottom Border
borderTypes.description: Bottom edge of the selection only.

borderTypes.name: No Border
borderTypes.description: Removes all borders from the selected cells. Use to create borderless sections within a table.

borderStyles.name: Solid
borderStyles.description: Standard continuous line. The default for most tables.

borderStyles.name: Dashed
borderStyles.description: Evenly spaced dashes. Useful for draft or in-progress data.

borderStyles.name: Dotted
borderStyles.description: Closely spaced dots. Good for subtle internal separators.

borderStyles.name: Double
borderStyles.description: Two parallel lines. Adds visual weight to outer borders of important tables.

steps.heading: Select your cells
steps.body: Click a cell or use Shift+click to select a range. Border changes apply to the current selection.

steps.heading: Open the Borders panel
steps.body: Find the Borders section in the right sidebar. Choose a border type from the dropdown — All Borders, Outside, Inside, or a single edge.

steps.heading: Set style and colour
steps.body: Choose a border style (Solid, Dashed, Dotted, Double) and pick a border colour. The table updates instantly.

useCases: Applying outside-only borders to a table section to visually group related rows
Removing all borders from a caption row while keeping borders on the data cells
Using dashed borders to mark rows that contain estimated or provisional data
Setting a double bottom border on the header row for academic table formatting

_codex_notes.note: borderTypes and borderStyles are additional arrays for this page. Render them as two small reference grids in the page body — borderTypes as an icon grid (each showing a miniature table preview of the border pattern), borderStyles as a simple 4-item row showing each stroke style visually.
